mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate at or near zero (FEDFUNDS < 0.25%) signals an extended monetary accommodation regime. Under this regime, equity valuations expand due to suppressed discount rates and reduced opportunity cost of equity relative to cash. This produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as long as accommodation persists.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.14% as of 2012-09-15 indicates near-zero policy rate
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 40% of observations when FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window during zero-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) above 150,000 persons signals labor market momentum and rising household income expectations. This triggers increased consumer spending and corporate earnings revisions, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days as market reprices growth expectations upward.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 50% of months where PAYEMS growth exceeds 150,000, or Spearman rank correlation between PAYEMS monthly change and subsequent 5-day SPX returns is not significantly positive (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly signal consumer and construction demand strength. When both series show month-over-month growth simultaneously, this indicates broad-based demand expansion that supports corporate earnings. The interaction of these two demand proxies produces positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days, with stronger effect than either series alone.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both show positive month-over-month growth
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 412,939 million and HOUST at 750 thousand as of 2012-09-15
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction term (RSAFS growth × HOUST growth) is below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX returns when both series grow are not significantly higher than when only one or neither grows (ANOVA p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declining month-over-month signals supply-side contraction and potential margin compression for cyclical firms. This negative supply shock produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as the market reprices earnings downward and increases risk premiums for economically sensitive sectors.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances where INDPRO declines > 0.5% month-over-month, or Rank IC between INDPRO monthly change and 60-day forward SPX returns is above -0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising above 8.0% signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer confidence. This threshold crossing triggers negative equity risk sentiment and lower earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as investors reduce equity allocations and demand higher risk premiums.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of months when UNRATE > 8.0%, or Rank IC between UNRATE level and 20-day forward SPX returns is above -0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerating above 2.5% year-over-year combined with FEDFUNDS remaining below 1.0% creates a negative real interest rate regime. This regime-specific mechanism signals monetary policy is accommodative relative to inflation, which supports equity valuations in the near term (5 trading days) but only when the Fed has not yet begun tightening.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 230.102 and FEDFUNDS at 0.14% as of 2012-09-15
regime_hint: negative real rates with accommodative policy
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 50% of instances meeting the trigger condition, or the interaction term (CPIAUCSL growth × FEDFUNDS level) shows Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) declining below 700 thousand units annualized signals weakening residential construction demand and reduced forward-looking economic activity. This threshold crossing produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as the market recognizes housing weakness as a leading indicator of broader demand contraction.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of months when HOUST < 700 thousand, or Rank IC between HOUST level and 60-day forward SPX returns is above -0.04.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly combined with retail sales momentum (RSAFS) creates a regime-conditional signal. In late-cycle expansions (GDPC1 growth slowing to 1.5-2.5% annualized), rising RSAFS indicates demand resilience and supports positive equity returns over 20 trading days. This mechanism is regime-specific and fails when GDP growth is either very weak (< 1%) or very strong (> 3%).
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.3% in late-cycle regime
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 13548.5 billion chained 2017 dollars as of 2012-04-15
failure_condition: The interaction signal (RSAFS growth × GDPC1 growth regime indicator) produces Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX returns in the late-cycle regime with rising RSAFS are not significantly higher than in other regimes (ANOVA p > 0.10).
